enonce.texte:
Read the short text below about gender discrimination and answer the questions that follow.

Text: A Story of Discrimination

Marie is a 14-year-old girl in Cameroon. She wants to become a pilot. However, her uncle tells her: "Flying is for men. You should stay home and learn to cook." Marie's parents disagree. They say: "Marie can be anything she wants. It is her right." Marie feels sad but she is determined. She works hard at school, especially in science and mathematics.

questions.texte: Identify the main problem Marie faces.
questions.reponse: Marie faces gender discrimination because her uncle believes that flying is only for men.

questions.texte: Explain why Marie's parents are important in this story.
questions.reponse: Marie's parents are important because they support her dream and believe in gender equality. They tell her that she can be anything she wants.

questions.texte: Analyse how Marie feels and what she decides to do about the situation.
questions.reponse: Marie feels sad because of her uncle's discrimination. However, she is determined. She decides to work hard at school, especially in science and mathematics, to achieve her dream of becoming a pilot.

intitule: Grammar Practice: Possessive Adjectives and Pronouns

enonce.texte: Complete the sentences with the correct possessive adjective (my, your, his, her, its, our, their) or possessive pronoun (mine, yours, his, hers, its, ours, theirs).

questions.texte: That bag is not _______. It's hers. (You)
The children are playing with _______ toys. (They)
The toys belong to the children. They are _______. (They)

enonce.texte: Write a short paragraph (5-7 sentences) about a situation of gender discrimination in your community. Use at least 3 possessive adjectives and 2 possessive pronouns.

questions.texte: Write your paragraph here. Remember to describe the situation, explain who is involved, and suggest a solution.
questions.reponse: Example answer: "In my community, some parents believe that education is only for boys. My neighbour's daughter, Amina, is a bright student. Her father, however, wants her to stay home. Amina's dream is to become a doctor. 'This is not fair,' she says. 'Education is mine and every girl's right.' With the help of her mother, she continues to go to school. I believe that everyone should have the same opportunity. The future belongs to all of us, not just a few."

enonce.texte:
Read the following situation and write a dialogue between two people discussing gender discrimination. Use possessive adjectives and pronouns in your dialogue.

Situation: You and your friend are discussing a job advertisement that says "Men only." Your friend thinks it's normal, but you believe it is discrimination.

questions.texte: Write a dialogue between you (Student A) and your friend (Student B). Include at least 8 exchanges.
questions.reponse:
Example dialogue:

Student A: Did you see the job advertisement for the manager position?

Student B: Yes, I did. It says "Men only." That's normal.

Student A: Normal? I don't think so. It's discrimination!

Student B: But some jobs are for men only. That's how it is.

Student A: No, that's not fair. Women can do any job. My mother is a manager at a bank.

Student B: Really? Your mother is a manager?

Student A: Yes, and her job is to lead the team. She is excellent at it.

Student B: I see your point. Maybe that advertisement is not right.

Student A: Exactly. Women deserve the same opportunities. This is our right as human beings.

intitule: Reading Comprehension: A Victim of Harassment

enonce.texte:
Read the following text and answer the questions.

Text: A Victim of Harassment

Paul is a student who is often bullied at school. Some students call him names because his clothes are old. They also tell him that he is not smart. Paul feels very sad. He wants to tell his teacher, but he is afraid. His sister, Chantal, encourages him. She says: "Paul, you must talk to the teacher. This is not your fault. It is the bullies' fault. Your feelings are important. You deserve respect." Paul decides to speak to his teacher. The teacher listens and promises to help.

questions.texte: Identify the type of discrimination Paul faces.
questions.reponse: Paul faces discrimination because he is bullied for having old clothes and being called not smart.

questions.texte: Describe Chantal's role in this story.
questions.reponse: Chantal plays the role of a supportive sister. She encourages Paul to speak up and tells him that it is not his fault.

questions.texte: Analyse the consequences of Paul's decision to speak to his teacher.
questions.reponse: By speaking to his teacher, Paul takes a brave step. The teacher promises to help, which means there may be an end to the bullying. It shows that speaking up is important to stop discrimination.

intitule: Grammar in Context: Rewrite the Paragraph

enonce.texte:
Read the paragraph below. Rewrite it using possessive adjectives and possessive pronouns to make it clearer and more correct.

Original text:

"John and Mary are students. John has a bicycle. The bicycle is John's. Mary has a book. The book is Mary's. The students have a teacher. The teacher is the students'. John and Mary like the teacher. John and Mary do the homework."

questions.texte: Rewrite the paragraph using possessive adjectives (my, your, his, her, its, our, their) and possessive pronouns (mine, yours, his, hers, its, ours, theirs).
questions.reponse:
Example answer:

"John and Mary are students. John has his bicycle. The bicycle is his. Mary has her book. The book is hers. The students have a teacher. The teacher is theirs. John and Mary like their teacher. They do their homework."

enonce.texte:
You are a member of a club that fights against gender discrimination. You want to create a poster to raise awareness in your school.

Write the text for the poster. It should contain:

  • A short title
  • At least 3 sentences explaining why discrimination is wrong
  • At least 2 possessive adjectives or pronouns
  • A call to action (what people should do)

questions.texte: Write the text for your poster.
questions.reponse:
Example answer:

Title: STOP DISCRIMINATION!

"Gender discrimination is unfair. Everyone has the right to education and a job. It is our responsibility to stand up for equality. Your voice can make a difference. We must respect each other regardless of gender. Join our club and fight against discrimination today!"

intitule: Listening Comprehension: Responding to an Announcement

enonce.texte:
Your school has announced a new policy against gender discrimination. Your teacher asks you to share your opinion about the policy.

Write a short speech (5-7 sentences) to present to the class. Use possessive adjectives and pronouns.

questions.reponse:
Example answer:

"Good morning everyone. I am happy about the new policy against gender discrimination. This policy is ours and we must respect it. My sister was once told she couldn't play football because she is a girl. That was unfair. Now, with this policy, we can stop such things. Your opinion matters, and so does mine. Let's all work together to make our school a fair place for everyone."
